libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set the GraalVM Native test build argument `-H:+AddAllCharsets` when tests exercise `okhttp3.internal.Util` or code paths that load it, because OkHttp 3.8.1 initializes UTF-32 charsets statically. For optional platform reachability coverage, keep tests self-contained with source-level stubs for Android and Jetty ALPN classes that OkHttp discovers reflectively rather than adding real Android or ALPN artifacts. Use the 3.8.x `Util.intersect(Util.NATURAL_ORDER, first, second)` signature if updating the existing compile-failing test.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6518 [Automation] javac compile fails for com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp:3.8.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding real Android or Jetty ALPN dependencies could change `Platform.get()` detection and make tests brittle.
Without `-H:+AddAllCharsets`, JVM compilation may pass while native-image verification can fail on charset initialization.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; OkHttp 3.8.1 is self-contained apart from transitive Okio, but meaningful native coverage should preserve the known charset/platform test setup.
